league.name: Serie A
league.description: Italy’s top division: tactical coaching, fierce derbies, and clubs tied to city identity. It is not “defence only” today — but games are often tighter, with managers adjusting shape minute by minute.
league.styleOfPlay: Organised defending and midfield blocks; wing-backs and three-at-the-back systems appear often. Teams protect shape first, then explode with individual skill. Set pieces and game management (fouls, cards, tempo) matter more than in some leagues.
famousClubs: AC Milan — seven European Cups, red-and-black tradition
Inter Milan — Nerazzurri stripes, shared San Siro with Milan
Juventus — record Serie A titles; black-and-white from Turin
Roma — capital club; passionate Curva Sud
Napoli — southern pride; Diego Maradona legacy
famousPlayers: Lautaro Martínez — Inter striker and Argentina star
Rafael Leão — AC Milan left winger
Nicolò Barella — Inter midfielder, Italy engine
league.historySummary: Serie A was among Europe’s strongest leagues in the 1980s–90s, known for world-class defenders and imports. Financial crises and match-fixing scandals hurt the brand, but Milan clubs and others rebuilt. Today it mixes old-school tactics with modern pressing sides.
rivalries: Derby della Madonnina — AC Milan vs Inter (same stadium)
Derby d’Italia — Juventus vs Inter (title-heavy history)
Derby della Capitale — Roma vs Lazio (Rome)
league.fanGuide: Pick a city derby first — Milan’s Madonnina is the easiest entry. Ultras and choreography differ by curva; respect that matchday is serious business. Watch how managers use five subs and tactical fouls; learn one formation (e.g. 3-5-2) to follow commentary.

teams.name: AC Milan
teams.shortHistory: AC Milan’s red and black stripes stand for Italian glamour and European pedigree. Arrigo Sacchi’s pressing teams and Paolo Maldini’s defending built eras fans still study. Seven European Cups put them among the continent’s elite — the Derby della Madonnina vs Inter defines the city.
teams.fanGuide: Rossoneri means “red and blacks.” They share San Siro with Inter — same stadium, different curvas. Curva Sud is Milan’s vocal end. On derby night Milan wears red/black and Inter wears blue/black; the whole city stops. Juventus away is Italy’s other historic grudge.
legends: Paolo Maldini — one-club defender; elegance and 25 years in rossonero
Franco Baresi — libero partner to Maldini; tactical genius at the back
Marco van Basten — Dutch striker; volleys and ruthlessness in the late 1980s
Kaká — Brazilian playmaker; Ballon d’Or winner who carried the 2007 Champions League
currentKeyPlayers: Rafael Leão — left wing; pace and Serie A star quality
Mike Maignan — goalkeeper; shot-stopping and playing out

teams.name: Inter Milan
teams.shortHistory: Inter’s blue and black stripes (“Nerazzurri”) come from merging clubs in 1908. They are known for structured defending, Italian grit, and big European nights. José Mourinho’s 2010 treble is the modern benchmark; recent Serie A titles put them back among Italy’s top two.
teams.fanGuide: Black and blue stripes; Curva Nord at San Siro is Inter’s heart. “Pazza Inter amala” captures the love-hate passion of supporting them. The Derby della Madonnina vs AC Milan splits Milan — same stadium, different ends. Juventus is the nationwide rivalry fans mention after the derby.
legends: Giuseppe Meazza — stadium namesake; striker who defined pre-war Italian football
Javier Zanetti — one-club captain; 19 years at Inter without a red card
Ronaldo Nazário — “Il Fenomeno”; explosive striker in the late 1990s
Marco Materazzi — tough defender; hero of the 2010 treble and 2006 World Cup
currentKeyPlayers: Lautaro Martínez — striker; pressing, link play, and goals
Nicolò Barella — midfielder; energy and line-breaking runs
Alessandro Bastoni — centre-back; left side of a back three
Yann Sommer — goalkeeper; reflex saves despite smaller stature

teams.name: Juventus
teams.shortHistory: Juventus hold the record for Serie A titles — the Old Lady’s black and white stripes symbolise Italian dominance, scandal, and rebuilds across decades.
teams.fanGuide: Black and white stripes; the Derby d’Italia vs Inter is a national grudge. Turin’s Derby della Mole vs Torino is the local fixture.
legends: Alessandro Del Piero — one-club symbol and free-kick artist
Gianluigi Buffon — goalkeeper for a generation
Michel Platini — French playmaker of the 1980s

teams.name: Napoli
teams.shortHistory: Napoli’s story is southern pride and Maradona’s 1987 scudetto — modern titles under Spalletti brought the city celebrations back to the streets.
teams.fanGuide: Sky blue shirts; Curva B is volcanic. The club is a symbol of Naples beyond football — learn Maradona’s name first.
legends: Diego Maradona — icon of the 1987 title
Ciro Ferrara — local defender
Gonzalo Higuaín — striker of title races

teams.name: AS Roma
teams.shortHistory: Roma carry the capital’s giallorossi identity — Francesco Totti’s one-club career defines modern fan memory alongside sporadic title challenges.
teams.fanGuide: Yellow and red; the Derby della Capitale vs Lazio splits Rome. Totti’s number 10 shirt is still sacred to Curva Sud.
legends: Francesco Totti — one-club captain and Serie A icon
Daniele De Rossi — local midfielder warrior
Gabriel Batistuta — Argentine striker

teams.name: Lazio
teams.shortHistory: Lazio’s sky blue comes from Greek heritage branding — the Rome derby and ultras culture make them one of Italy’s most polarising clubs.
teams.fanGuide: Light blue shirts; Curva Nord at the Olimpico. Derby vs Roma is the first fixture Roman fans explain.
legends: Paolo Maldini — brief youth spell before Milan
Alessandro Nesta — elegant defender
Ciro Immobile — modern goalscorer

players.name: Kevin De Bruyne
players.quickFact: Set the Premier League single-season assists record in 2019–20 — a benchmark for modern playmakers.
players.playingStyle: World-class passer who dictates tempo with vision, whipped crosses, and long-range shooting.
quizHints: Belgian international who rebuilt his reputation in the Bundesliga after a brief London spell.
Genk academy product who also wore Wolfsburg green before the Etihad.
Whips crosses from the right half-space — ginger beard, supreme vision, and a cannon of a right foot.

players.name: Luka Modrić
players.quickFact: Ballon d’Or winner in 2018 — the first player outside Messi or Ronaldo to win it in over a decade.
players.playingStyle: Metronome midfielder with outside-foot passes, press resistance, and legendary stamina.
quizHints: Croatian midfielder who fled war as a child and grew up in a hotel as a refugee.
White Hart Lane years before a decade-plus in Madrid white.
Outside-of-the-foot passes and metronome tempo — number 10 for La Roja’s neighbors.

players.name: Rafael Leão
players.quickFact: His motorcycle-throttle celebration nods to riding culture — he is known for languid dribbles that suddenly explode into top speed.
players.playingStyle: Gangly speedster who glides past defenders with long strides and cool finishing.
quizHints: Portuguese left winger who wears Rossoneri red and black at San Siro.
Ligue 1 cameo at Lille before Milan made him a Serie A star.
2021–22 Scudetto-winning side’s standout attacker on the left flank.

players.name: Mike Maignan
players.quickFact: PSG never gave him a Ligue 1 appearance — Lille and Milan turned him into one of Europe’s top shot-stoppers.
players.playingStyle: Athletic shot-stopper with quick feet and confident distribution under pressure.
quizHints: French keeper born in Cayenne, French Guiana.
Won Ligue 1 at Lille before replacing a Cameroonian international at Milan.
Known for quick feet and playing out from the back in Serie A — number 16.

players.name: Lautaro Martínez
players.quickFact: His bull celebration — hands on hips, charging — became one of Argentina’s most copied goal rituals after 2022.
players.playingStyle: Pressing forward who links play, drops deep, and finishes with power and movement off the shoulder.
quizHints: Argentine striker who left Racing’s light blue and white for Nerazzurri black and blue.
World Cup co-top scorer in Qatar alongside his country’s greatest number 10.
Inter captain who leads the line in Serie A — bull celebration after goals.

players.name: Nicolò Barella
players.quickFact: Cagliari sold their homegrown midfielder to Inter — he later ran the engine room of Italy’s Euro 2020 winners.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box dynamo with relentless energy, line-breaking passes, and emotional leadership.
quizHints: Sardinian-born Italian with trademark blonde-streaked hair.
Small stature, huge lungs — covers every blade at San Siro.
Euro 2020 champion who wears Inter’s number 23 in blue and black.

players.name: Alessandro Bastoni
players.quickFact: Left-footed centre-back in a back three — Atalanta groomed him before Inter made him a Serie A title pillar.
players.playingStyle: Modern defender who steps into midfield, slides tackles, and sprays diagonal passes.
quizHints: Italian defender who plays on the left side of a defensive trio in blue and black.
Bergamo youth product before a big move across Lombardy to San Siro.
Progressive passes and stepping into midfield are his calling cards.

players.name: Yann Sommer
players.quickFact: At 1.83 m he is short for a modern elite keeper — yet ranked among Europe’s best reflex shot-stoppers for years at Gladbach.
players.playingStyle: Reflex keeper with excellent penalty saving and calm handling in one-on-ones.
quizHints: Swiss international who spent nearly a decade in green and white at Mönchengladbach.
Basel boy before Bundesliga fame and a 2023 move to San Siro.
Replaced a Cameroonian number one when joining Inter’s Serie A title-winning side.

players.name: Adrien Rabiot
players.quickFact: France midfielder whose box-to-box power featured for Paris Saint-Germain and Juventus before an AC Milan move in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Powerful central midfielder who drives through pressure, combines in transition, and covers ground defensively.
quizHints: France international midfielder known for long strides and carrying the ball.
Played for Paris Saint-Germain and Juventus before Milan.
AC Milan midfielder whose first name is Adrien and surname is Rabiot.

players.name: Alexis Saelemaekers
players.quickFact: Belgium winger whose direct running at AC Milan made him a Serie A wide outlet with crossing and work rate on the right.
players.playingStyle: Hard-running winger who attacks the byline, delivers crosses, and defends with commitment on the flank.
quizHints: Belgium international winger who plays on the right for AC Milan.
Direct wide player known for pace, crossing, and pressing.
Attacker whose first name is Alexis and surname is Saelemaekers.

players.name: Christopher Nkunku
players.quickFact: France forward who exploded at RB Leipzig before Chelsea and AC Milan, and was part of the 2018 World Cup-winning squad.
players.playingStyle: Fluid forward who drops between lines, combines quickly, and finishes with both feet in the box.
quizHints: France attacker who won the World Cup in Russia in 2018.
RB Leipzig goal machine before Premier League and Serie A moves.
Christopher with full name — versatile forward, not a left-back.

players.name: Fikayo Tomori
players.quickFact: England centre-back who won the Champions League with Chelsea before becoming an AC Milan defensive leader in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Pacey centre-back who defends aggressively in recovery runs, steps into midfield, and reads through-balls quickly.
quizHints: England defender who came through Chelsea’s academy.
Won the Champions League at Chelsea before starring in Italy with Milan.
AC Milan centre-back whose first name is Fikayo and surname is Tomori.

players.name: Ruben Loftus-Cheek
players.quickFact: England midfielder who won the Champions League with Chelsea and rebuilt his career at AC Milan as a powerful Serie A runner.
players.playingStyle: Powerful midfielder who carries the ball through lines, wins duels, and supports attacks with late box arrivals.
quizHints: England international midfielder who came through Chelsea’s academy.
Tall ball-carrier known for driving runs and physical presence in midfield.
Midfielder whose first name is Ruben and surname is Loftus-Cheek.

players.name: Santiago Giménez
players.quickFact: Mexico centre-forward who scored heavily for Feyenoord before AC Milan and leads El Tri’s line with movement in the box.
players.playingStyle: Mobile striker who attacks the penalty spot, presses defenders, and finishes with either foot.
quizHints: Mexico striker in green who moved to AC Milan from the Netherlands.
Feyenoord goal scorer — not Raúl Jiménez the veteran forward.
Santiago who finishes with headers and sharp near-post runs.

players.name: Strahinja Pavlović
players.quickFact: Serbia centre-back who moved from Red Bull Salzburg to AC Milan and brings aerial dominance to the Orlovi back line.
players.playingStyle: Physical centre-back who defends the box, wins aerial duels, and carries the ball out calmly.
quizHints: Serbia centre-back in red who plays for AC Milan.
Tall defender from Salzburg — not Strahinja Tanasijević in MLS.
Strahinja at Milan who wins headers and steps into midfield.

players.name: Sead Kolasinac
players.quickFact: Bosnia and Herzegovina left-sided defender linked to La Zmajeva in FootyBrain, with long Serie A tenure at Arsenal, Schalke, and Marseille before Atalanta.
players.playingStyle: Athletic defender who overlaps on the flank, tackles aggressively, and delivers set-piece threat.
quizHints: Bosnia-Herzegovina defender in the linked national pool.
Atalanta full-back — not Ahmedhodžić at centre-back.
Sead nicknamed "the Tank" for physical defending for Bosnia.

players.name: Hakan Çalhanoğlu
players.quickFact: Turkey midfielder whose free kicks and passing became Inter Milan’s creative engine after Bundesliga and Milan spells.
players.playingStyle: Deep playmaker who dictates tempo, strikes dead balls, and switches play with weighted passes from midfield.
quizHints: Turkey international famous for set-piece delivery and long-range shooting.
Played for AC Milan before becoming Inter’s deep-lying playmaker.
Inter Milan midfielder whose first name is Hakan.

players.name: Henrikh Mkhitaryan
players.quickFact: Armenia midfielder whose creativity at Dortmund, Manchester United, and Roma continued at Inter Milan in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Crafty midfielder who finds space between lines, supplies through balls, and finishes with subtle technique.
quizHints: Armenia’s all-time great midfielder who played in Germany, England, and Italy.
Left-footed playmaker known for assists, vision, and intelligent movement.
Midfielder whose first name is Henrikh and surname is Mkhitaryan.

players.name: Petar Sučić
players.quickFact: Croatia midfielder who emerged in the Eredivisie and became an Inter Milan prospect with passing range and composure.
players.playingStyle: Smooth midfielder who circulates possession, breaks lines with passes, and keeps tempo with intelligent positioning.
quizHints: Croatia youth international midfielder at Inter Milan.
Technical central player known for passing and control in tight spaces.
Midfielder whose first name is Petar and surname is Sučić.

players.name: Piotr Zielinski
players.quickFact: Poland midfielder who won Serie A with Napoli before Inter Milan and remains a creative hub for the Biało-czerwoni.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder who arrives late, shoots from distance, and threads passes between lines.
quizHints: Poland playmaker in white and red who starred for Napoli in Serie A.
Inter Milan midfielder — not Robert Lewandowski the striker.
Left-footed no. 10 type known for late runs into the box.

players.name: Andrea Cambiaso
players.quickFact: Italy full-back whose energy and dribbling made him a Juventus wide option after breaking through at Bologna and Genoa.
players.playingStyle: Dynamic full-back who overlaps constantly, drives inside with the ball, and presses aggressively to win it back.
quizHints: Italy international full-back who can play left or right sides.
Quick defender who carries the ball forward from wide areas.
Juventus full-back whose first name is Andrea and surname is Cambiaso.

players.name: Bremer
players.quickFact: Brazil centre-back who became one of Serie A’s standout defenders before joining Juventus.
players.playingStyle: Aggressive centre-back who attacks headers, steps into challenges, and defends with front-foot intensity.
quizHints: Built his Italian reputation across town at Torino.
Brazilian defender known for physical duels and aerial strength.
One-name centre-back in black and white stripes for Juventus.

players.name: Dušan Vlahović
players.quickFact: Serbia centre-forward who broke through at Fiorentina before Juventus and leads the line for the Orlovi with powerful finishing.
players.playingStyle: Target striker who holds up play, attacks crosses, and finishes with strength and placement.
quizHints: Serbia striker in red kit who plays for Juventus.
Tall centre-forward — not Filip Kostić on the left wing.
Dušan who finishes with power inside the penalty area.

players.name: Federico Gatti
players.quickFact: Italy centre-back who rose from lower-league football to become a Juventus starter known for aerial duels and direct defending.
players.playingStyle: Aggressive centre-back who attacks aerial balls, steps out boldly, and keeps defending simple and physical.
quizHints: Italy international centre-back who progressed from Serie B to Juventus.
Physical defender known for heading and aggressive duels.
Centre-back whose first name is Federico and surname is Gatti.

players.name: Filip Kostić
players.quickFact: Serbia left winger whose crossing and work rate at Eintracht Frankfurt and Juventus made him a staple wide outlet for the Orlovi.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who hugs the touchline, crosses early, and presses with high intensity.
quizHints: Serbia left-sided player in red who crossed for Frankfurt in the Europa League.
Juventus wide midfielder — not Dušan Vlahović the striker.
Filip who delivers whipped crosses from the left.

players.name: Jonathan David
players.quickFact: Canada striker who scored prolifically for Lille and Gent before Juventus and leads the Les Rouges line alongside Alphonso Davies.
players.playingStyle: Mobile centre-forward who finds space in the box, links play, and finishes with both feet.
quizHints: Canadian centre-forward linked to Canada’s national team.
Juventus striker — not Alphonso Davies the left-back.
Jonathan who wears no. 10 for Canada in the FootyBrain pool.

players.name: Lloyd Kelly
players.quickFact: England left-back who won trophies with Brighton and Newcastle before becoming a Juventus defensive option in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Composed left-sided defender who defends space well, steps into midfield, and builds attacks with tidy passing.
quizHints: England international defender who came through Bristol Rovers.
Left-footed centre-back or full-back comfortable in possession.
Defender whose first name is Lloyd and surname is Kelly.

players.name: Weston McKennie
players.quickFact: United States midfielder whose energy and versatility made him a Juventus squad regular after Schalke breakthrough.
players.playingStyle: Dynamic midfielder who covers ground, supports transitions, and adds physical presence in both boxes.
quizHints: USA international who broke through at Schalke in the Bundesliga.
Box-to-box midfielder known for late runs, pressing, and aerial duels.
Juventus midfielder whose first name is Weston and surname is McKennie.

players.name: Adam Marusic
players.quickFact: Montenegro right-back who became a Lazio defensive regular with disciplined positioning and reliable wide defending.
players.playingStyle: Dependable right-back who stays compact, defends the channel, and supports attacks with simple overlaps.
quizHints: Montenegro international who plays right-back in Serie A.
Long-time Lazio full-back known for steady defending.
Lazio defender whose first name is Adam and surname is Marusic.

players.name: Fisayo Dele-Bashiru
players.quickFact: England midfielder who came through Manchester City’s academy and later became a Lazio box-to-box runner in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Dynamic midfielder who presses hard, carries the ball forward, and supports both defensive screens and attacking runs.
quizHints: England youth international midfielder with Premier League roots.
Energetic central player who covers ground and arrives late in the box.
Midfielder whose first name is Fisayo and surname is Dele-Bashiru.

players.name: Gustav Isaksen
players.quickFact: Denmark winger whose direct running at Midtjylland and Bologna earned him a Lazio move as a right-sided goal threat in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who attacks defenders at speed, cuts inside to shoot, and threatens goals from wide areas.
quizHints: Denmark international winger who played in Denmark and Italy before Lazio.
Right-footed wide forward known for pace and shooting from the right.
Attacker whose first name is Gustav and surname is Isaksen.

players.name: Matteo Cancellieri
players.quickFact: Italy winger who broke through at Lazio with direct dribbling and finishing after coming through Roma’s youth system.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger who attacks defenders one-on-one, drives into the box, and finishes with quick shots across goal.
quizHints: Italy youth international winger who developed at Roma before Lazio.
Right-footed wide forward known for pace and cutting inside to shoot.
Attacker whose first name is Matteo and surname is Cancellieri.

players.name: Pedro
players.quickFact: Spanish winger from Barcelona’s golden era who won major trophies with club and country.
players.playingStyle: Two-footed wide forward who times runs behind defenders and finishes calmly from either side.
quizHints: Played alongside Messi, Xavi, and Iniesta during Barcelona’s peak years.
Later brought experience to Chelsea, Roma, Lazio, and Spain squads.
One-name Spanish winger currently listed with Lazio.

players.name: Alessandro Buongiorno
players.quickFact: Italy centre-back who developed at Torino and became a Napoli defensive signing known for pace and proactive defending.
players.playingStyle: Modern centre-back who defends space aggressively, carries into midfield, and reads through-balls with recovery speed.
quizHints: Italy international centre-back who came through Torino.
Quick defender who steps out of the line to intercept and carry forward.
Napoli centre-back whose first name is Alessandro and surname is Buongiorno.

players.name: Billy Gilmour
players.quickFact: Scotland international defensive midfielder in the linked Scotland national squad.
players.playingStyle: Technical defensive midfielder who dictates tempo and breaks up play.
quizHints: Scottish midfielder linked to Scotland.
Deep-lying playmaker — not McGinn the box-to-box runner.
Plays in the Premier League.

players.name: Frank Anguissa
players.quickFact: Cameroon midfielder whose ball carrying and press resistance helped Napoli after he became a Premier League standout at Fulham.
players.playingStyle: Dynamic central midfielder who carries past lines, wins duels, and links defence to attack with powerful dribbles.
quizHints: Cameroon international midfielder who starred at Fulham before Napoli.
Known for carrying the ball through midfield under pressure.
Napoli midfielder whose first name is Frank and surname is Anguissa.

players.name: Giovanni Di Lorenzo
players.quickFact: Italy right-back who captained Napoli during their Serie A title era and became a reliable Italy international.
players.playingStyle: Experienced right-back who defends steadily, supports attacks with overlaps, and organizes teammates with vocal leadership.
quizHints: Italy international right-back who captained a Serie A title-winning Napoli side.
Veteran full-back known for consistency, crossing, and leadership.
Defender whose first name is Giovanni and surname is Di Lorenzo.

players.name: Romelu Lukaku
players.quickFact: Belgium’s record goal scorer and a powerful striker with major spells in England and Italy.
players.playingStyle: Power striker who pins defenders, attacks space, and finishes quickly with his left foot.
quizHints: Broke through young at Anderlecht before Premier League moves.
Belgian forward known for strength, left-footed finishing, and direct runs.
Centre-forward listed with Napoli in this FootyBrain dataset.

players.name: Scott McTominay
players.quickFact: Scotland midfielder who became a goal threat at Napoli after Manchester United, adding aerial power to the national midfield.
players.playingStyle: Physical midfielder who carries forward, wins aerial duels, and finishes from late runs.
quizHints: Scotland midfielder in dark blue who left Manchester United for Napoli.
Tall box-to-box player — not Billy Gilmour the deeper no. 6.
Scott with a Scottish surname who arrives late in the box.

players.name: Vanja Milinković-Savić
players.quickFact: Serbia goalkeeper who starred at Torino before Napoli and backs up the national team with strong shot-stopping.
players.playingStyle: Reactive goalkeeper with strong reflexes who organises the defence and plays out when safe.
quizHints: Serbia goalkeeper in red kit who joined Napoli from Torino.
Tall Serb keeper with a long double surname — not a centre-back.
Vanja who spreads well and commands crosses in the box.

players.name: Angeliño
players.quickFact: Spain left-back whose crossing and attacking runs featured for Manchester City, RB Leipzig, and later Roma in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Attacking left-back who pushes high, crosses early, and combines with wingers in tight overloads.
quizHints: Spain international left-back who came through Manchester City’s academy.
Known for overlapping runs and whipped deliveries from the left.
AS Roma full-back known by the single name Angeliño.

players.name: Artem Dovbyk
players.quickFact: Ukraine striker whose Girona goal glut earned a big move to Roma as a classic penalty-box finisher.
players.playingStyle: Penalty-box striker who attacks crosses, finishes first-time, and stays central to maximize shooting chances.
quizHints: Top scorer in La Liga with Girona before joining Roma.
Ukraine international centre-forward known for poaching in the box.
AS Roma striker whose first name is Artem.

players.name: Bryan Cristante
players.quickFact: Italy midfielder whose size and versatility let Roma use him as a destroyer, recycler, or emergency option in attack.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder who covers ground, wins second balls, and keeps team shape with disciplined positioning.
quizHints: Tall Italy international who has played for Roma across multiple managers.
Former Benfica and Atalanta midfielder before settling in the capital.
Roma player whose first name is Bryan and surname is Cristante.

players.name: Evan Ferguson
players.quickFact: Ireland striker who broke through at Brighton as a teenage goalscorer before a Roma move in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Powerful young striker who links play with his back to goal, attacks crosses, and finishes with calm one-touch strikes.
quizHints: Ireland international centre-forward who debuted young in the Premier League.
Tall striker known for hold-up play and finishing inside the box.
Forward whose first name is Evan and surname is Ferguson.

players.name: Evan Ndicka
players.quickFact: France centre-back whose pace and left-footed defending made him a Frankfurt standout before Roma in Serie A.
players.playingStyle: Athletic centre-back who covers ground, steps out to intercept, and builds play with composed left-foot passes.
quizHints: France international centre-back who played in Germany with Eintracht Frankfurt.
Quick left-footed defender comfortable carrying out from the back.
Centre-back whose first name is Evan and surname is Ndicka.

players.name: Zeki Çelik
players.quickFact: Turkey right-back who starred at Lille before Roma and provides defensive reliability and crossing for Ay Yıldız.
players.playingStyle: Defensive full-back who tackles firmly, supports attacks, and crosses from deep positions.
quizHints: Turkey right-back in red and white who plays for Roma.
Lille and Roma full-back — not Ferdi Kadıoğlu on the left.
Zeki who defends aggressively and delivers cut-backs.

players.name: Ismaël Koné
players.quickFact: Canada midfielder who broke through at Watford and Marseille before Serie A with Sassuolo in the linked Canada squad.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder who drives with the ball, breaks lines, and supports both phases.
quizHints: Canadian midfielder linked to Canada’s national team.
Sassuolo central player — not Jonathan David the striker.
Ismaël who carries the ball forward from midfield for Canada.
